title: New Ecommerce Tools: May 12, 2026
description:
This week: predictive AI, autonomous marketing, site builders, omnichannel fulfillment, programmatic ads, embedded payments, cross-border commerce, and more.
The post New Ecommerce Tools: May 12, 2026 appeared first on Practical Ecommerce.

title: VTEX revenue, GMV grow double digits in Q1
description:
Cloud-based ecommerce platform provider VTEX reported double-digit year-over-year growth in both revenue and gross merchandise volume (GMV) in its fiscal Q1 2026, which ended March 31. Co-founder and co-CEO Geraldo Thomaz Jr. said VTEX has placed an emphasis on growing its artificial intelligence (AI) suite. It has extended its AI product suite across its commerce, […]
The post VTEX revenue, GMV grow double digits in Q1 appeared first on Digital Commerce 360.

title: eBay rejects GameStop’s acquisition offer
description:
Online marketplace eBay has publicly rejected an acquisition offer from GameStop’s CEO that would have seen the retailer attempt to purchase the company. “We have concluded that your proposal is neither credible nor attractive,” said Paul Pressler, eBay’s chairman of its board of directors, in a formal response. Nine days prior, GameStop CEO Ryan Cohen […]
The post eBay rejects GameStop’s acquisition offer appeared first on Digital Commerce 360.

title: Amazon launches Amazon Now in U.S. for 30-minute delivery
description:
Amazon announced on May 12 that millions of its U.S. customers can order products for delivery in 30 minutes or less through Amazon Now. The company will rely on micro-fulfillment centers to enable faster delivery speeds. In October 2025, it announced delivery in timeframes as short as 15 minutes in the United Arab Emirates using […]
The post Amazon launches Amazon Now in U.S. for 30-minute delivery appeared first on Digital Commerce 360.

title: OroCommerce partners with Intellias, introduces a new integration with iPaaS.com
description:
The business-to-business (B2B) ecommerce platform OroCommerce announced new collaborations with iPaas.com and Intellias in separate offerings for its manufacturing clients, as well as wholesalers and distributors. The integration and partnership were both detailed through public announcements in May. They cover enterprise resource planning (ERP), customer relationship management (CRM) and other digital commerce operations for OroCommerce […]
The post OroCommerce partners with Intellias, introduces a new integration with iPaaS.com appeared first on Digital Commerce 360.

title: Kraft Heinz’s biggest portfolio campaign to date celebrates America250
description: The CPG giant is uniting several of its top brands to capitalize on cookout season as it ramps up marketing spend to support its turnaround.

title: Foot Locker debuts year-round basketball brand platform under new CMO
description: Brett O’Brien, previously chief sports officer at PepsiCo, was appointed by the retailer after it was acquired by Dick’s Sporting Goods in September.

title: The dangerous gap between AI output and actual understanding
description:
AI makes it easier than ever to produce impressive work. It’s also making it harder to tell who actually understands the strategy behind it.
The post The dangerous gap between AI output and actual understanding appeared first on MarTech.

title: Last-click attribution rewards the wrong work in an AI-first world
description:
AI is making marketing influence harder to track. Here’s how to avoid misleading attribution data and make better investment decisions.
The post Last-click attribution rewards the wrong work in an AI-first world appeared first on MarTech.

title: CMOs are buying AI that their organization isn’t ready for
description:
A new Gartner survey finds most marketing organizations are investing heavily in AI while lacking the processes and infrastructure to scale it successfully.
The post CMOs are buying AI that their organization isn’t ready for appeared first on MarTech.

title: #213: AI Answers - What AI Should Never Do, Enterprise Scaling, Governing AI & Navigating IT Roadblocks
description:
Leadership wants AI everywhere. IT security can't keep up. Marketing is racing ahead while legal and finance dig in. And every week brings another story of an AI agent doing something nobody told it to do. Paul Roetzer and Cathy McPhillips answer 15 listener questions on how to actually move organizations forward and where the real opportunities lie for individuals, SMBs, and enterprises right now.

00:00:00 — Intro

00:07:01 — How do you move a company out of AI policy paralysis?

00:08:54 — How should regulated, hands-on teams introduce AI?

00:12:30 — When companies are stuck, what tends to get them moving?

00:15:00 — Should IT security evolve to adopt AI quicker or should businesses slow down?

00:17:43 — What changes an AI-skeptic employee's mind?

00:21:18 — How should early-career professionals prioritize what to learn?

00:23:29 — Is there a point where you should stop learning and start building?

00:27:29 — Where do companies get stuck scaling AI across departments?

00:29:4

title: Mail Metro Media shifts ad strategy toward PMPs and fewer ads as it unifies stack
description: Mail Metro Media wants to drive 300% PMP growth over the next three years as part of plans to turn a high-volume digital direct business into an outcomes shop.

title: Ad tech is lining up behind OpenAI (it’s been here before)
description: OpenAI needs ad tech. Ad tech knows it won’t always.

title: [AINews] The End of Finetuning
description: a quiet day lets us reflect on whither finetuning

title: Medicare’s new payment model is built for AI, and most of the tech world has no idea
description: There is no governmental mechanism to pay for an AI agent that monitors a patient between visits, calls to check in, coordinates a housing referral, or makes sure someone picks up their medication. ACCESS creates that mechanism for the first time.
